suggested_action: No position now; re-rate when Iveco financing structure is confirmed and TMF Holdings AGM vote outcome is known
entry_price_source: S&P Capital IQ Key Stats export
rating_cap: Filter 4 (serial-acquirer/very-large-deal) + Filter 6 (unaligned owner borderline) enforce maximum Watchlist until Iveco financing resolved and minority-holder protection evidence exists
variant_perception_summary: No proven variant perception. The Iveco acquisition binary risk and governance concerns are publicly known. Market appears to be rationally pricing the deal risk at 1.7% implied FCF CAGR — conservative but explainable. No proprietary data point distinguishes this analysis from consensus.
what_everyone_knows: TMCV is India's largest CV manufacturer (35.7% share), recently listed as a pure-play, generating genuine cash returns (FCF Rs12,438 cr, ROCE 72%), with an improving balance sheet and 550 bps of EBITDA margin expansion over three years.
what_is_priced_in: The Iveco acquisition risk and associated balance-sheet transformation. The reverse-DCF at 13% WACC shows the current price implies only ~1.7% FCF CAGR over ten years — approximately 8-9 percentage points below India nominal GDP growth — suggesting the market is already applying a large discount for the Iveco binary.
what_market_may_be_missing: The compound effect of the TMF Holdings merger on reported earnings quality may not be receiving enough attention. A Rs2,418 cr non-cash FVTPL loss already reduced reported PAT by 80% in FY26. If both Iveco bridge loan and TMF merger close simultaneously in FY27, the reported earnings reset will be severe and consensus likely resets sharply downward.
killer_risk: Iveco Group acquisition closes on full EUR 3.875 bn bridge-loan terms with no equity component — equity value falls from ~Rs380-430 base case to approximately Rs66/share on the intrinsic DCF

kill_criteria.condition: Iveco closes fully debt-funded with no equity component
kill_criteria.what_it_means: Equity value falls to ~Rs66/share; net debt rises to ~Rs33,000 cr (~3.2x EBITDA); annual interest Rs3,000-4,000 cr; FY27 consensus cut 20-30%; every balance-sheet metric resets
kill_criteria.monitor_via: NSE/BSE exchange intimations on deal close + financing terms; SEBI LODR Note updates; management bridge-loan disclosure on earnings call

kill_criteria.condition: TMF Holdings merger passes AGM (June 29, 2026) AND FVTPL losses repeat at Rs2,000+ cr in FY27
kill_criteria.what_it_means: Confirms promoter willingness to transfer group liabilities to minority holders permanently; reported PAT noise is structural; governance discount becomes permanent
kill_criteria.monitor_via: AGM vote results (NSE/BSE scrutinizer report post June 29); FY27 reported PAT vs normalised PAT gap

kill_criteria.condition: Q1 FY27 Underlying EBITDA margin falls below 11%
kill_criteria.what_it_means: FY23-FY26 margin expansion is reversing; full-year consensus EPS Rs19.17 becomes unreachable even without Iveco; operating-leverage thesis broken
kill_criteria.monitor_via: Q1 FY27 results press release (late July/early August 2026); Underlying EBITDA margin line

kill_criteria.condition: India CV TIV contracts >10% in FY27
kill_criteria.what_it_means: Operating de-leverage triggers; EPS collapses 40-45% per the -10% volume sensitivity; combined with Iveco interest, EPS could go deeply negative
kill_criteria.monitor_via: Monthly SIAM wholesale data; MHCV retail registration trends; diesel prices at pump

kill_criteria.condition: Post-Iveco NCD maturities refinanced at 100+ basis points above existing WAC (~7.3-7.5%)
kill_criteria.what_it_means: First tangible evidence that post-Iveco leverage has degraded credit access; rating agency action likely follows
kill_criteria.monitor_via: September/December 2026 NCD refinancing terms; CRISIL/ICRA rating watch announcements

forecast_ledger.prediction: Q1 FY27 Underlying EBITDA margin will print below FY26's 12.3%
forecast_ledger.time_window: Q1 FY27 results — late July / early August 2026
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Management confirmed Q1 commodity impact significantly higher than Q4's 100 bps; seasonal H1 softness; no contractual pass-through mechanism
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Q1 results show Underlying EBITDA margin at or below 12.0%
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Q1 Underlying EBITDA margin at or above 12.3% — commodity absorption fully offset by pricing and volume

forecast_ledger.prediction: Iveco Group acquisition closes in Q2 FY27 (July-September 2026)
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: CFO guided Q2 close; France and Spain approvals pending; activist hedge fund holds 6-7% of Iveco; all lender consents obtained to date
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: NSE/BSE exchange intimation announcing deal completion
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Management announces deal termination, withdrawal, or material delay beyond Q3 FY27

forecast_ledger.prediction: FY27 normalised EPS consensus will be revised downward post-Q1 results
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Stale Apr-27-2026 consensus assumes +20.5% EPS growth; does not model Iveco bridge loan; Q1 commodity headwind pre-confirmed by management
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Post-Q1-results broker revisions visible in Capital IQ; FY27E normalised EPS falls below Rs17.00
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: No broker revisions; FY27 consensus holds above Rs19.00 after Q1 actuals

forecast_ledger.prediction: TMF Holdings amalgamation will face material institutional opposition at 2nd AGM (June 29, 2026)
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Promoter at 42.56% cannot carry the 75% special-resolution threshold alone; FIIs hold 19%; IiAS recommendation not yet published
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: IiAS or major proxy advisor recommends voting against; AGM vote count shows less than 75% in favour
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Resolution passes with 75%+ and no material institutional opposition

forecast_ledger.prediction: Net cash position will be eliminated by H1 FY28 at the latest
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Iveco deal consideration Rs41,691 cr vs current net cash (company-defined) Rs13,713 cr; bridge loan terms confirmed at Euribor +102.5 bps; equity raise web-sourced at ~EUR1 bn
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: FY28 interim results show net debt on consolidated balance sheet
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Deal does not close; or monetisation of Tata Capital stake fully offsets deal consideration with no new borrowing

forecast_ledger.prediction: TMCV will not pay a dividend at the same Rs4/share level in FY27
forecast_ledger.evidence_today: Post-Iveco annual interest Rs3,000-4,000 cr compresses FCF; management has not committed to a dividend policy post-Iveco
forecast_ledger.confirmation_trigger: Board recommends zero or reduced FY27 dividend at Q4 FY27 board meeting
forecast_ledger.falsification_trigger: Board recommends Rs4/share or higher FY27 dividend, confirming FCF adequacy post-Iveco

business-model.verdict: Average business — worth deeper work only at right price; Filter 4 and Filter 5 tripped; no hard disqualifier

earnings.verdict: Mixed earnings setup; earnings quality 72/100; consensus setup 30/100 (capped); red-flags verdict Critical concerns

balance-sheet-survival.verdict: Fortress balance sheet (pre-Iveco only); solvency strength 82/100; liquidity runway 88/100; overall usefulness capped at 75 (covenant gap)

management-governance.verdict: Standard/mixed; Governance Score 51 (Weak band); 9 High red flags, 0 Critical; Filters 4 and 6 tripped

valuation.verdict: Modestly undervalued; base fair value Rs380-430; bear Rs300-346; event-risk floor Rs66; confidence capped at 50 (missing 07_scenario-and-fair-value)

catalyst.verdict: Dated, evidenced near-term catalysts; calendar skews bearish-to-neutral; Iveco close is untimable binary; timing visibility 52/100

red_flags.trigger: Iveco EUR3.8bn acquisition — zero ROIC target, synergy, or leverage ceiling disclosed; 3.3x book equity
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.116; FQ4 FY26 Transcript Q&A p.9; May 13, 2026

red_flags.trigger: CLAUDE.md S24 Filter 4 — serial-acquirer/very-large-deal at 3.3x book equity with bare strategic-fit language; CapAlloc max 50; GovRisk floor 60
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.116; SEBI LODR Note 7, May 13, 2026

red_flags.trigger: RPT outflows 8.2% of revenue — above 5% High threshold; dominated by Tata Cummins engine procurement
TMF Holdings amalgamation pending — Rs2,478 cr negative PAT contribution without quantified minority economics disclosure
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, Note 45; Note 11; SEBI LODR Note 11, May 13, 2026

red_flags.trigger: Loans/ICDs to subsidiaries Rs2,925 crore (23% of consolidated net worth)
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, Standalone Notes 9-10, p.395-414

red_flags.trigger: CLAUDE.md S24 Filter 6 borderline — TMF merger transfers NBFC losses (Rs2,478 cr FVTPL drag) to listed entity at promoter direction; ShFriendliness max 55
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, Board's Report, p.117; Note 45; Q3 FY26 press release, Jan 29, 2026

red_flags.trigger: Bonus scorecard rewards volumes and absolute EBITDA (size metrics); no ROIC, EPS, FCF/share, or TSR metric named; PSU vesting criteria undisclosed
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, CG Report, p.233; Board's Report, p.121

red_flags.trigger: Noncyclical revenue KPI unverifiable from audited segment notes; TMF merger financial impact not quantified before shareholder vote
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, MD&A, p.215; Q3 FY26 press release, Jan 29, 2026

red_flags.trigger: Recurring FVTPL addback creates 94.7% gap between reported PAT (Rs3,030 cr) and normalised PAT (~Rs5,900 cr); item is recurring by nature not genuinely exceptional
red_flags.source: FY26 Annual Report, MD&A, p.215; SEBI LODR Note 8, May 13, 2026

missing_data: Iveco acquisition financing structure — equity vs debt split; bridge loan final terms; post-deal net debt/EBITDA ceiling — single highest-value missing data point
Financial covenant thresholds (net-worth floor, leverage cap, coverage floor) for all active financing facilities — would lift covenant headroom from Not Assessable to precise number
Post-Q4 FY26 analyst estimate revisions to FY27 consensus (EstimatesReport.xls extraction failed; full revision series unavailable)
Company Comparable Analysis peer multiples (XLS extraction failed; peer comps web-sourced and unverified throughout)
SEBI PIT/SAST individual insider-transaction disclosures since November 2025 listing — would confirm whether any insider bought TMCV shares at market
TMF Holdings FVTPL portfolio composition and expected directional bias in FY27 — critical for estimating FY27 reported PAT
Iveco EBITDA margin and revenue profile — needed to model consolidated post-deal earnings contribution
Post-deal HoldCo/OpCo capital structure and covenant package for the EUR3.875 bn bridge facility
notes: Entry price Rs369.15 is available from S&P Capital IQ Key Stats export (2026-06-07); paper treatment is Watchlist (no trade, track opportunity cost) per frameworks/DECISION_LEDGER.md S3. Risk/reward of -0.23 reflects the probability-weighted expected return being negative (-4.4%), making the expected outcome a small loss at current price. The 30d review (2026-07-07) is most critical — AGM outcome (June 29) and early Iveco regulatory signals should be visible by then. The 90d review (2026-09-05) will capture Q1 FY27 results and the September NCD maturity outcome.
